question: Set 1 Winner: Kraus vs Akugue
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Sinja Kraus and Noma Noha Akugue in the Roland Garros, Qualification WTA, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Kraus” if Sinja Kraus wins the first set. It will resolve to “Akugue” if Noma Noha Akugue wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.
groupItemTitle: Roland Garros, Qualification WTA: Sinja Kraus vs Noma Noha Akugue Set 1 Winner

events.title: Roland Garros, Qualification WTA: Sinja Kraus vs Noma Noha Akugue
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Sinja Kraus and Noma Noha Akugue in the Roland Garros, Qualification WTA, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Sinja Kraus' if Sinja Kraus advances against Noma Noha Akugue.

This market will resolve to 'Noma Noha Akugue' if Noma Noha Akugue advances against Sinja Kraus.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sinja Kraus held a clear edge in the Roland Garros qualifying matchup against Noma Noha Akugue on clay, driven by her higher WTA ranking near No. 92 and seeding as the No. 2 qualifier. Kraus arrived with strong recent clay form, including a three-set win over Celine Naef to reach the second round, while Akugue sat around No. 154-164 with limited recent success against top-tier opponents. Their head-to-head stood tied at 1-1 entering the match, though Kraus’s experience in Grand Slam qualifying and superior baseline consistency on the slower surface shaped trader consensus. The three-set battle ultimately highlighted Kraus’s ability to recover after dropping the opener, underscoring her edge in longer rallies typical of French Open clay.

question: Set 1 Winner: Norrie vs Navone
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Mariano Navone in the Geneva Open, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Norrie” if Cameron Norrie wins the first set. It will resolve to “Navone” if Mariano Navone wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP match statistics.

events.title: Geneva Open: Cameron Norrie vs Mariano Navone
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Mariano Navone in the Geneva Open, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Cameron Norrie' if Cameron Norrie advances against Mariano Navone.

This market will resolve to 'Mariano Navone' if Mariano Navone advances against Cameron Norrie.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cameron Norrie entered the 2026 Geneva Open clay-court event as the No. 3 seed and a semifinalist from the prior year, but recent form on the surface raised questions after a second-round exit at the Italian Open. Argentine Mariano Navone, a proven clay specialist ranked No. 42, carried strong momentum with multiple straight-set wins earlier in the tournament and held a 2-0 head-to-head edge, including a prior straight-sets victory over Norrie. Navone’s aggressive baseline game and backhand down-the-line consistency aligned well with the slow red clay, while Norrie’s results showed vulnerability against similar stylistic matchups. Traders weighed these surface and recency factors heavily in assessing implied probabilities for the Round of 32 contest.

question: Norrie vs. Navone: Set 1 Games O/U 10.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Cameron Norrie and Mariano Navone in the Geneva Open, originally scheduled for May 20, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 11. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

question: AI data center in space by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any orbital data center is successfully launched by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Orbital data center” refers to any spacecraft, satellite, or equivalent technology carrying computing infrastructure that is launched into Earth’s orbit for the purpose of providing data-center, cloud-computing, or artificial intelligence computing services and which includes at least 100 data-center-grade AI accelerators, GPUs, TPUs, or substantially equivalent compute processors, (e.g. NVIDIA H100 GPUs, Google TPUs, or equivalent or successor chips).

“Successfully launched” refers to any launch which successfully places a qualifying orbital data center into Earth’s orbit.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: AI data center in space by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any orbital data center is successfully launched by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Orbital data center” refers to any spacecraft, satellite, or equivalent technology carrying computing infrastructure that is launched into Earth’s orbit for the purpose of providing data-center, cloud-computing, or artificial intelligence computing services and which includes at least 100 data-center-grade AI accelerators, GPUs, TPUs, or substantially equivalent compute processors, (e.g. NVIDIA H100 GPUs, Google TPUs, or equivalent or successor chips).

“Successfully launched” refers to any launch which successfully places a qualifying orbital data center into Earth’s orbit.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent hardware demonstrations and regulatory filings have shaped trader views on timelines for orbital AI compute. Starcloud's November 2025 launch of a satellite carrying an NVIDIA H100 GPU marked the first training of a large language model in space, validating radiation-hardened inference and fine-tuning, though it remains a single-node prototype. Google’s Project Suncatcher, detailed in late 2025, targets two-satellite TPU tests with optical inter-satellite links by early 2027 alongside Planet Labs. SpaceX’s post-merger FCC filing for up to one million data-center satellites, plus announced 2027 demonstrator goals, adds momentum, yet analysts note current orbital costs remain several times higher than terrestrial equivalents. Key near-term catalysts include Starcloud-2’s planned multi-GPU launch later in 2026 and additional FCC comment periods that could accelerate or constrain constellation scale.

question: Will the highest temperature in Milan be 25°C or below on July 11?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Malpensa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Malpensa Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/it/milan/LIMC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast runs from major models indicate Milan’s July 11 maximum will likely fall between 30–34 °C, with ensemble spreads reflecting modest uncertainty over daytime cloud cover and boundary-layer mixing across the Po Valley. High-pressure ridging continues to favor clear skies and strong insolation, yet small timing shifts in any afternoon convection or wind could trim or boost the peak by 1–2 °C at official stations such as Malpensa. Traders therefore assign the highest implied probabilities to 30–32 °C outcomes, consistent with the narrow historical range for early-July maxima under similar synoptic patterns and the limited window before final observations are recorded.

question: Will Tampa Bay Buccaneers win the 2026 NFC South?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the NFC South division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Pro Football: NFC South Champion
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the NFC South division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The NFC South remains one of the league's least predictable divisions entering 2026, with no team holding clear separation after the Panthers captured the title in 2025. Tampa Bay enters as the slight trader favorite thanks to continuity under Todd Bowles despite losing longtime contributors Mike Evans and Lavonte David, while New Orleans shows promise behind new quarterback Tyler Shough and additions such as Travis Etienne Jr. Atlanta and Carolina sit close behind on the strength of young cores and aggressive offseasons, though the Falcons face questions at quarterback and the Panthers draw a demanding schedule. Recent roster turnover across all four clubs, combined with the absence of a proven frontrunner, sustains the bunched pricing that reflects broad uncertainty heading into training camp.

question: Will Movsar Evloev fight Alexander Volkanovski next?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Alexander Volkanovski is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Alexander Volkanovski is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Volkanovski is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/).

events.title: Who will Alexander Volkanovski fight next?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Alexander Volkanovski is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alexander Volkanovski’s repeated public endorsements of undefeated Movsar Evloev as the clear featherweight number-one contender have anchored market consensus following his unanimous-decision title defense against Diego Lopes at UFC 325 in February. The Australian champion has cited Evloev’s record and recent performances while targeting an August or September return, aligning with typical UFC matchmaking for top-ranked contenders seeking title shots. Evloev’s positioning above other ranked featherweights reinforces the implied probability reflected in current pricing. Realistic shifts could occur if the UFC prioritizes a different matchup for scheduling or promotional reasons, an injury sidelines either fighter, or an interim contender like Jean Silva or Lerone Murphy secures a standout win that alters the division hierarchy before an official announcement.

question: Will Elisha Ellison win by KO or TKO?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Elisha Ellison defeats Gable Steveson at UFC 329: McGregor vs. Holloway 2, scheduled for July 11, 2026, by KO or TKO, including referee stoppage, doctor stoppage, or corner stoppage. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond July 25, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC 329: Gable Steveson vs. Elisha Ellison (Heavyweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Gable Steveson" if Gable Steveson is officially declared the winner of the fight against Elisha Ellison at UFC 329: McGregor vs. Holloway 2, scheduled for July 11, 2026.

It will resolve to "Elisha Ellison" if Elisha Ellison is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond July 25, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gable Steveson's Olympic gold medalist wrestling pedigree and 3-0 MMA record have driven overwhelming trader consensus for a dominant debut win over 5-2 veteran Elisha Ellison at UFC 329 on July 11. The heavyweight prelim bout at T-Mobile Arena shifted from the main card after lineup adjustments tied to the McGregor-Holloway 2 headliner, reflecting Steveson's status as a high-upside prospect transitioning from NFL and WWE pursuits. Ellison, coming off an early UFC loss and known for finishing power, has publicly outlined a spoiling strategy, yet faces steep stylistic disadvantages against Steveson's elite grappling. Recent betting lines have settled in the -2000 to -3200 range, underscoring the market's view of limited realistic paths for an upset despite the short-notice nature of some heavyweight debuts.

question: Will JetBlue Airways announce bankruptcy before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether or not the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, including a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or any other individual or team that officially represents the company.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: David Møller Wolfe: 1+ shots
description: In the upcoming FIFA World Cup game between Norway and England, scheduled for July 11 at 5:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if David Møller Wolfe records more than 0.5 shots within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. This market will resolve to "No" if David Møller Wolfe records 0.5 shots or fewer within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Shots are counted only if credited to David Møller Wolfe in the official match statistics. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. If David Møller Wolfe is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead. All markets will settle based on the official final result as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. Revisions to officially declared final scores made after market resolution will not be accounted for in determining the outcome.

events.title: Norway vs. England - Player Props
events.description: Player prop markets for the FIFA World Cup game between Norway and England, scheduled for July 11 at 5:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Norway’s quarter-final clash with England at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami on July 11 features Erling Haaland’s exceptional scoring form—seven goals in four World Cup matches—driving interest in his goal, shots, and anytime scorer props against an England side led by Harry Kane. Norway’s camp has been disrupted by a spreading illness bug with flu-like symptoms affecting multiple players after their Round of 16 celebrations, potentially limiting attacking output and clean-sheet potential. Extreme Miami heat (feels-like temperatures near 44°C) raises fatigue and postponement concerns that could suppress overall goal tallies and alter substitution patterns. England’s strong favorite status (trader-implied probabilities around 53%) and possession edge favor Kane and supporting attackers, while yellow-card accumulation risks for several Three Lions players add context for booking-related props. Official injury reports and starting lineups will be decisive closer to kickoff.
